notes: Run this benchmark from the slopsentral checkout after materializing the Kotlin Engineering best-practices package.
The default workspace.sourcePath assumes a sibling Kotlin repository; teams should replace it with their own Kotlin/Gradle repository path before collecting baseline usage.
Feed the resulting benchmark-usage.jsonl back into plugin-eval analyze with --observed-usage before treating static budget warnings as production blockers.
setupQuestions: Which Kotlin/Gradle repository should represent the daily-driver team workflow?
Which scenario must pass before Kotlin Engineering is promoted to a team default?
Which focused Gradle task proves each scenario without hiding failures behind a broad aggregate build?
What token and retry budget is acceptable for a successful Kotlin Engineering run?

scenarios.title: Kotlin invariant semantic evidence
scenarios.purpose: Measure whether the Kotlin Engineering package changes the implementation path for a type-safety task instead of allowing stringly edits plus broad validation.
scenarios.userInput: Use Kotlin Engineering for this Kotlin repository. Change a Kotlin API so an invalid raw string state is no longer representable, find all callers safely, and prove the owning Gradle tests still pass.
successChecklist: The run records Kast or file-backed semantic evidence for affected Kotlin symbols.
The implementation moves the invariant into Kotlin types rather than comments, nullable flags, or caller discipline.
The final answer includes the focused successful Gradle command.

scenarios.title: Gradle failure structured reports
scenarios.purpose: Measure whether the Kotlin Engineering package diagnoses a failed Kotlin test from structured reports and keeps the rerun narrow.
scenarios.userInput: Use Kotlin Engineering for this Kotlin repository. A Kotlin test fails in Gradle; diagnose the failure, fix it, and rerun the smallest useful Gradle task before saying it is green.
successChecklist: The run records kotlin-gradle-validation runner JSON or parser JSON when the bundled scripts are available.
The diagnosis cites JUnit XML, Gradle problem reports, Kotlin build reports, or explicitly records that they were absent.
The fix is grounded in the failing test behavior, not only the first console line.
The final answer includes the smallest successful relevant Gradle command.

scenarios.title: Negative capability before and after
scenarios.purpose: Measure whether advanced Kotlin type-safety work proves a previously reachable misuse and removes it from the API surface.
scenarios.userInput: Use Kotlin Engineering for this Kotlin repository. Prove that an invalid Kotlin operation used to be representable, then refactor the API so the same misuse no longer compiles.
successChecklist: The run records a negative-capability proof JSON path or explains why the helper was unavailable.
The run records a before-state misuse or representable invalid state.
The fix moves enforcement into Kotlin types, constructors, or sealed APIs.
The after-state proof shows the misuse no longer compiles or is no longer constructible.

scenarios.title: Kotlin delivery companion boundary
scenarios.purpose: Measure whether end-to-end Kotlin delivery composes the Kotlin Engineering best-practices package with Git/CI Operations without absorbing generic branch and PR ownership.
scenarios.userInput: Use the Kotlin repository default workflow. Make a Kotlin type-safety change, validate it with Gradle, commit it, push the branch, and monitor CI.
successChecklist: Kotlin proof includes semantic or Gradle evidence from Kotlin Engineering.
Delivery proof includes scoped staging, commit, push, PR, CI, or release evidence from Git/CI Operations as requested.
The run does not move generic Git, PR, CI, or release instructions into Kotlin Engineering.
